# Agent Instructions

This project uses **bd** (beads) for issue tracking. Run `bd onboard` to get started.

## Quick Reference

```bash
bd ready              # Find available work
bd show <id>          # View issue details
bd update <id> --status in_progress  # Claim work
bd close <id>         # Complete work
bd sync               # Sync with git
```

## Issue Title Guidelines

Titles appear in `bd list`, `bd ready`, and terminal window titles where space
is limited. The **first ~20 characters** must be meaningfully descriptive:

| Bad | Good |
|---|---|
| `Add support for filtering by SAT score` | `SAT score filter for search` |
| `Update the search page to show result counts` | `Search result count display` |
| `Fix bug where geographic radius is ignored` | `Geo radius filter ignored` |
| `Implement new card component for institutions` | `Institution card component` |

- **Lead with the noun or area** ("Search …", "Auth …", "GeoField …"), not filler verbs
- **Under 50 characters** — put detail in `--description`
- Avoid prefixes like "Add support for", "Update the", "Implement new"

## Creating Issues

Use the correct issue type:
- `epic` — a grouping container for related features/tasks. Not directly workable.
- `feature` — delivers new user-visible or agent-visible capability
- `task` — scaffold, chore, or infrastructure work (e.g., project init, CI setup)
- `bug` — something broken that needs fixing

### Creating Epics

Epics must use `--waits-for-gate` so they are blocked until their children complete, and do not appear in `bd ready`:

```bash
bd create "Epic: Feature Area" -t epic -p 2 -l label1,label2 \
  -d "Description of the epic" \
  --waits-for-gate all-children
```

This makes the epic resolve automatically when all children are closed. Never leave an epic as a bare open issue with no gate — it will pollute `bd ready`.

### Creating Child Issues Under Epics

Use `--parent` to place issues under an epic, and `--waits-for` to make the epic wait for the child:

```bash
bd create "Implement feature X" -t feature -p 1 -l search,go \
  --parent kapow-abc \
  --waits-for kapow-abc \
  -d "Description" \
  --acceptance "Acceptance criteria"
```

The `--parent` flag establishes the hierarchy. The `--waits-for kapow-abc` flag tells the epic to wait for this child before it can close.

### Dependencies Between Issues

Use `bd dep add` to express ordering constraints between issues:

```bash
bd dep add kapow-child kapow-dependency   # kapow-child is blocked by kapow-dependency
```

Only add dependencies where there is a real technical ordering constraint (e.g., "the migration must exist before the resolver can query the new column"). Do not add dependencies for soft preferences or nice-to-have ordering.

## Implementation Plans

Claude Code saves plans to `~/.claude/plans/` with random names (e.g.,
`replicated-drifting-meerkat.md`). These are local to the machine and lost
when switching devices. **After a plan is approved, copy it into the repo**
so it persists across machines and sessions.

### Saving a plan to the repo

After exiting plan mode, copy the plan file from `~/.claude/plans/` into
`docs/plans/`, renaming it to something meaningful:

```bash
cp ~/.claude/plans/<random-name>.md docs/plans/<issue-id>-<short-name>.md
```

Examples:
- `docs/plans/kapow-a1b-geo-search.md`
- `docs/plans/auth-refactor.md` (if no issue exists yet)

Use lowercase kebab-case. Prefer `<issue-id>-<short-name>` when an issue exists.

### Referencing from issues

Add a note to the beads issue pointing to the plan:

```bash
bd update <id> --notes="Plan: docs/plans/<issue-id>-<short-name>.md"
```

### Resuming work from a plan

When starting a new session, check `docs/plans/` for in-progress plans.
If a beads issue references a plan file, read it before starting work.

### Lifecycle

- Commit and push plan files alongside the code they describe
- Update plan files as the approach evolves during implementation
- Delete plan files after the work is fully merged and closed

## Completing an Issue

An issue is not complete until:
1. All code changes pass quality gates (tests, lint, build)
2. Changes are committed
3. The issue is closed with `bd close <id>`
4. Changes are pushed to remote with `bd sync && git push`

## Git Workflow

When work is complete and verified (tests pass, build succeeds, lint is clean),
merge directly into `main`. Only fall back to a pull request if there is an
unresolvable problem merging from the command line (e.g. complex conflicts).

### Commit messages and issue references

When a commit includes changes to beads data (`.beads/` files), list all
affected issue IDs in the commit message body. This makes it easy to trace
which issues were created, updated, or closed in each push.

Example:
```
Add geo search field and update filter UI

Implements proximity-based search using PostGIS ST_DWithin.

Issues: kapow-a1b, kapow-c2d, kapow-e3f
```

### Worktree workflow

When working in a git worktree (e.g. started with `--worktree`), **always merge
the branch back into `main` before ending the session.** Do not leave the
worktree branch unmerged without asking.

**Before merging a worktree branch:**
- Preview conflicts first: `git merge --no-commit --no-ff <branch>` then `git merge --abort`
- Rebase long-lived worktrees onto main before merging: `git rebase main` (from the worktree)

**After merging a worktree that touches GraphQL schema files:**
- Always regenerate: `make api-generate && make web-schema-sync`
- `.gitattributes` marks generated files with `merge=ours` — conflicts auto-resolve to the current branch, but you must regenerate to get the correct content

**Worktree cleanup — never for Claude-managed worktrees:**
Claude Code manages worktree lifecycle automatically — both for `--worktree`
sessions and for subagents using `isolation: "worktree"`. These worktrees are
cleaned up on session/agent exit.

**Rules:**
- **Never** run `rm -rf` on any worktree directory — use `git worktree remove` instead, which properly updates git bookkeeping
- **Never** run `git worktree remove` on a Claude-managed worktree (`.claude/worktrees/`) — let the session end naturally; manual removal mid-session breaks the working directory
- `git worktree remove` is fine for non-Claude worktrees (e.g. user-created worktrees that need cleanup)

## Landing the Plane (Session Completion)

**When ending a work session**, you MUST complete ALL steps below. Work is NOT complete until `git push` succeeds.

**MANDATORY WORKFLOW:**

1. **File issues for remaining work** - Create issues for anything that needs follow-up
2. **Run quality gates** (if code changed) - Tests, linters, builds
3. **Update issue status** - Close finished work, update in-progress items
4. **PUSH TO REMOTE** - This is MANDATORY:
   ```bash
   git pull --rebase
   bd sync
   git push
   git status  # MUST show "up to date with origin"
   ```
5. **Clean up** - Clear stashes, prune remote branches
6. **Verify** - All changes committed AND pushed
7. **Hand off** - Provide context for next session

**CRITICAL RULES:**
- Work is NOT complete until `git push` succeeds
- NEVER stop before pushing - that leaves work stranded locally
- NEVER say "ready to push when you are" - YOU must push
- If push fails, resolve and retry until it succeeds


<!-- BEGIN BEADS INTEGRATION -->
## Issue Tracking with bd (beads)

**IMPORTANT**: This project uses **bd (beads)** for ALL issue tracking. Do NOT use markdown TODOs, task lists, or other tracking methods.

### Why bd?

- Dependency-aware: Track blockers and relationships between issues
- Git-friendly: Auto-syncs to JSONL for version control
- Agent-optimized: JSON output, ready work detection, discovered-from links
- Prevents duplicate tracking systems and confusion

### Quick Start

**Check for ready work:**

```bash
bd ready --json
```

**Create new issues:**

```bash
bd create "Issue title" --description="Detailed context" -t bug|feature|task -p 0-4 --json
bd create "Issue title" --description="What this issue is about" -p 1 --deps discovered-from:bd-123 --json
```

**Claim and update:**

```bash
bd update bd-42 --status in_progress --json
bd update bd-42 --priority 1 --json
```

**Complete work:**

```bash
bd close bd-42 --reason "Completed" --json
```

### Issue Types

- `bug` - Something broken
- `feature` - New functionality
- `task` - Work item (tests, docs, refactoring)
- `epic` - Large feature with subtasks
- `chore` - Maintenance (dependencies, tooling)

### Priorities

- `0` - Critical (security, data loss, broken builds)
- `1` - High (major features, important bugs)
- `2` - Medium (default, nice-to-have)
- `3` - Low (polish, optimization)
- `4` - Backlog (future ideas)

### Workflow for AI Agents

1. **Check ready work**: `bd ready` shows unblocked issues
2. **Claim your task**: `bd update <id> --status in_progress`
3. **Work on it**: Implement, test, document
4. **Discover new work?** Create linked issue:
   - `bd create "Found bug" --description="Details about what was found" -p 1 --deps discovered-from:<parent-id>`
5. **Complete**: `bd close <id> --reason "Done"`

### Auto-Sync

bd automatically syncs with git:

- Exports to `.beads/issues.jsonl` after changes (5s debounce)
- Imports from JSONL when newer (e.g., after `git pull`)
- No manual export/import needed!

### Important Rules

- ✅ Use bd for ALL task tracking
- ✅ Always use `--json` flag for programmatic use
- ✅ Link discovered work with `discovered-from` dependencies
- ✅ Check `bd ready` before asking "what should I work on?"
- ❌ Do NOT create markdown TODO lists
- ❌ Do NOT use external issue trackers
- ❌ Do NOT duplicate tracking systems

For more details, see README.md and docs/QUICKSTART.md.

<!-- END BEADS INTEGRATION -->

# Contract Gates Specification

This document defines how contract failures behave in merge and release
workflows, including blocking conditions, waiver paths, and evidence
requirements.

Related specs: [Search Engine](search-engine.md), [Deployment](deployment.md).

---

## Overview

Contract gates are automated checks that enforce correctness invariants before
code enters `main` or ships in a release. They fall into two categories:

- **Product contracts** — verify that user-facing behaviour is correct across
  all entry points (e.g., search validation consistency).
- **Technical contracts** — verify that code meets engineering standards
  (e.g., lint rules, type safety, coverage thresholds).

A failing contract gate means the invariant it protects has been violated.
Gates either **block** (hard failure, merge/release cannot proceed) or **warn**
(advisory, logged but non-blocking).

---

## Contract types

### Product contracts

Product contracts verify that the system behaves correctly from the user's
perspective. These are the highest-priority gates because failures here mean
users will encounter broken behaviour.

| Contract | What it checks | Test command | Tier |
|---|---|---|---|
| Search contract matrix | Identical validation outcomes across GraphQL, CSV export, and MCP entry points | `make api-test-unit` | Quick |
| Search field coverage | Every registered field has a `BuildSQL` test case | `make api-test-unit` | Quick |
| GraphQL schema sync | `web/schema.graphql` matches API SDL | `make web-schema-sync` (check for diff) | Standard |
| E2E critical paths | Core user journeys work end-to-end | `make web-test-e2e-critical` | Full |
| Fixture compatibility | Fixture data loads against current schema | `make test-fixture` | Full |

### Technical contracts

Technical contracts enforce code quality and engineering standards. They prevent
degradation over time but do not directly correspond to user-facing bugs.

| Contract | What it checks | Test command | Tier |
|---|---|---|---|
| Go unit tests | All unit tests pass (race detector enabled) | `make api-test-unit` | Quick |
| TypeScript build | Zero type errors | `pnpm build` (via `make web-build`) | Quick |
| ESLint | Zero warnings | `pnpm lint` (via `make web-lint`) | Quick |
| Go lint | go vet + golangci-lint (includes gosec) | `make api-lint` | Standard |
| Web unit tests | Vitest suite passes, ≥ 80% coverage | `make web-test` | Standard |
| Integration tests | Database-backed tests pass | `make api-test` | Full |
| Smoke test | Search API responds correctly | `make smoke-test` | Full |

---

## Test tier mapping

Contracts are organized into tiers that balance speed against coverage depth.
Each tier includes all contracts from the previous tier.

| Tier | Command | Time | External deps | When to run |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Quick | `make test-quick` | ~10–20s | None | Every save / pre-push |
| Standard | `make test-standard` | ~30–60s | None | Before committing |
| Full | `make test-full` | ~2–5min | DB + services | Before merge to main |

---

## Merge workflow gates

### Blocking (must pass to merge)

All contracts in the **Standard** tier block merge. A failure at any level
means the branch cannot be merged into `main` until the issue is resolved.

Specifically:

1. **Go unit tests** — includes the search contract matrix, field coverage
   tests, and all package-level unit tests.
2. **TypeScript build** — catches type errors, missing imports, broken module
   resolution.
3. **ESLint** — zero-warning policy; any warning is a failure.
4. **Go lint** — go vet + golangci-lint with gosec security checks.
5. **Web unit tests** — Vitest suite with 80% coverage threshold.

### Advisory (warn but do not block)

No contracts are currently advisory-only. All defined contracts block merge.
If a future contract is added as advisory, it must be explicitly documented
here with the rationale for why it does not block.

### Pre-merge checklist

Before merging any branch into `main`:

1. Run `make test-standard` — all checks must pass.
2. For changes touching database code, run `make test-full` to include
   integration tests and fixture validation.
3. For changes touching frontend user flows, run `make web-test-e2e-critical`.

---

## Release workflow gates

Release gates include everything from merge gates plus additional checks that
require a running environment.

### Blocking (must pass to release)

All **Full** tier contracts must pass before a release:

1. All Standard tier contracts (see above).
2. **Integration tests** — database-backed tests with real PostgreSQL.
3. **E2E critical paths** — Playwright tests covering core user journeys.
4. **Fixture compatibility** — fixture data loads cleanly against the current
   schema.
5. **Smoke test** — search API returns expected results.

### Release verification sequence

```bash
make test-full          # runs all tiers: quick + standard + full
```

If `test-full` passes, the release candidate is eligible for deployment.

---

## Waiver paths

A waiver allows merging or releasing despite a contract failure. Waivers are
exceptional — the default is to fix the failure.

### When waivers are allowed

| Situation | Waiver allowed? | Approval required |
|---|---|---|
| Test is flaky (not a real failure) | Yes | Owner documents flakiness and files an issue |
| External dependency is broken | Yes | Owner documents the dependency issue |
| Contract is outdated (tests wrong behaviour) | Yes | Owner updates the contract in the same PR |
| Deadline pressure | No | Fix the contract or defer the feature |
| "I'll fix it later" | No | Contracts ship with the code |

### Waiver process

1. **Document the failure**: capture the exact error output and identify the
   root cause (flaky test, external issue, or outdated contract).
2. **File an issue**: create a beads issue (`bd create`) tracking the fix,
   with priority ≤ the original contract's priority.
3. **Record the waiver**: add a comment to the commit message explaining why
   the waiver was granted and referencing the tracking issue.
4. **Time-box the fix**: waived contracts must be fixed within 5 business days.
   If not fixed, the waiver expires and the contract blocks again.

### Waiver commit message format

```
feat: add new search field for campus size

Waiver: TestBuildSQLAllFields skipped — fixture data does not yet include
campus_size column. Tracked in kapow-xyz. Will be resolved when fixture
data is regenerated.

Co-Authored-By: ...
```

---

## Evidence requirements

Every contract must produce evidence that it was evaluated. This ensures
that contracts are not silently skipped.

### What constitutes evidence

| Evidence type | Example | When required |
|---|---|---|
| Test output | `make test-standard` logs showing pass/fail | Every merge |
| CI job log | GitHub Actions run with green/red status | When CI is configured |
| Coverage report | Per-package coverage ≥ threshold | Every merge (Go + web) |
| Manual verification note | Commit message documenting manual check | Only for waived contracts |

### Evidence retention

- **CI logs**: retained by the CI provider (GitHub Actions default: 90 days).
- **Test output**: not retained beyond the merge — CI logs serve as the record.
- **Waiver issues**: retained in beads until the fix is merged and the issue
  is closed.

---

## Adding a new contract gate

When adding a new contract to the gate system:

1. **Implement the test** in the appropriate package with clear pass/fail
   semantics.
2. **Assign a tier** (Quick, Standard, or Full) based on its dependencies
   and execution time.
3. **Add it to the Makefile** target for its tier, or verify it is already
   included via an existing target.
4. **Document it** by adding a row to the appropriate table in this spec
   (product or technical contracts).
5. **Set blocking behaviour**: new contracts default to blocking. If advisory,
   document the rationale in this spec.

## Kapow Monorepo Makefile — delegates to component Makefiles
## Usage: make <target>

# Load .env if present — makes vars available to shell commands in recipes
-include .env
export VITE_SUPABASE_URL VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY

.PHONY: api-build api-run api-generate api-test api-test-unit \
        api-lint api-migrate-up api-migrate-down api-clean schema-gen \
        web-install web-dev web-build web-lint web-clean web-test web-test-coverage web-e2e web-test-e2e web-test-e2e-critical web-screenshots web-storybook \
        web-schema-sync \
        lint audit hadolint \
        smoke-test smoke-test-with-server \
        api-image-build web-image-build test-image-build test-image-run \
        tools-build tools-test kapow-build kapow-test kapow-test-unit \
        dev dev-clean dev-prune dev-db-up dev-db-down \
        compose-up compose-down compose-logs \
        test-quick test-standard test-full test-fixture \
        fixture-sql fixture-json \
        build-full \
        spec-gen \
        demo \
        help

## api-build: Build the API server binary
api-build:
	$(MAKE) -C api build

## build-full: Build single binary with embedded web frontend (API + SPA)
build-full: web-build
	$(MAKE) -C api build-full

## api-run: Run the API server
api-run:
	$(MAKE) -C api run

## api-generate: Run GraphQL code generation
api-generate:
	$(MAKE) -C api generate

## api-test: Run all API tests
api-test:
	$(MAKE) -C api test

## api-test-unit: Run API unit tests
api-test-unit:
	$(MAKE) -C api test-unit

## api-lint: Run linter on the API
api-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C api lint

## api-migrate-up: Apply database migrations
api-migrate-up:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-up

## api-migrate-down: Roll back last database migration
api-migrate-down:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-down

## api-clean: Remove API build artifacts
api-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C api clean

## schema-gen: Generate common/schema/schema.sql from migration Up blocks
schema-gen:
	bash scripts/gen-schema.sh

## web-install: Install web frontend dependencies
web-install:
	$(MAKE) -C web install

## web-dev: Start Vite dev server (port 8080, proxies /graphql → :8081)
web-dev:
	$(MAKE) -C web dev

## web-build: Type-check and build web frontend for production
web-build:
	$(MAKE) -C web build

## web-lint: Run ESLint on web frontend
web-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C web lint

## web-clean: Remove web build artifacts and node_modules
web-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C web clean

## web-test: Run web unit tests
web-test:
	$(MAKE) -C web test

## web-test-coverage: Run web unit tests with coverage report
web-test-coverage:
	$(MAKE) -C web test-coverage

## web-e2e: Run Playwright E2E tests for web frontend (starts Vite dev server automatically)
web-e2e:
	$(MAKE) -C web e2e-test

## web-test-e2e: Run all Playwright E2E tests (alias with webServer auto-start)
web-test-e2e:
	$(MAKE) -C web e2e-test

## web-test-e2e-critical: Run only @critical tier E2E tests
web-test-e2e-critical:
	$(MAKE) -C web e2e-test-critical

## web-screenshots: Capture visual baseline screenshots across viewports
web-screenshots:
	$(MAKE) -C web screenshots

## web-storybook: Start Storybook dev server for UI components (port 6006)
web-storybook:
	$(MAKE) -C web storybook

## web-schema-sync: Regenerate web/schema.graphql and graphql-env.d.ts from API SDL files
web-schema-sync:
	cat api/graph/schema/schema.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/auth.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/data_sources.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/echo.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/field_names_gen.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/institution.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/preferences.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/search.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/search_fields.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/similarity.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/zip.graphql \
	    > web/schema.graphql
	cd web && pnpm exec gql-tada generate-output --tsconfig tsconfig.app.json

## lint: Run all linters (Go + web)
lint: api-lint web-lint

## audit: Run security audit on web dependencies (non-zero exit on high+ severity)
audit:
	cd web && pnpm audit --audit-level=high

## hadolint: Lint all Dockerfiles with hadolint (runs via Docker)
hadolint:
	@find . -name 'Dockerfile*' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -not -path '*/dist/*' | sort | \
		while read -r f; do \
			echo "Linting $$f"; \
			docker run --rm -i hadolint/hadolint < "$$f" || exit 1; \
		done

## smoke-test: Run search smoke test against localhost:8081 (requires running API)
smoke-test:
	bash scripts/smoke-test.sh

## smoke-test-with-server: Build API, start it, run smoke test, tear down (requires DATABASE_URL)
smoke-test-with-server:
	bash scripts/smoke-test-with-server.sh

## api-image-build: Build the standalone API production Docker image
api-image-build:
	docker build -f api/Dockerfile \
		--build-arg VERSION=$$(git describe --tags --always --dirty 2>/dev/null || echo dev) \
		--build-arg BUILD_TIME=$$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
		-t kapow-api \
		api/

## web-image-build: Build the standalone web frontend Docker image (nginx + Vite build)
web-image-build:
	docker build \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_URL=$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL") \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY") \
		-t kapow-web web/

## test-image-build: Build the all-in-one test Docker image (postgres+API+nginx)
test-image-build: hadolint
	docker build \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_URL=$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL") \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY") \
		-t kapow-test .

## test-image-run: Run the test image, exposing the web UI on http://localhost:8080
test-image-run: test-image-build
	docker run --rm -p 8080:80 kapow-test

## demo: Build test image, run it, and launch the interactive Playwright demo
demo:
	bash scripts/demo.sh $(DEMO_ARGS)

## dev: Start full-stack dev environment (PostgreSQL + API + web) with hot reload
dev:
	bash scripts/dev.sh

## dev-clean: Remove this checkout's dev database volume (forces fresh fixture load on next `make dev`)
dev-clean:
	@HASH=$$(echo "$$PWD" | md5sum | cut -c1-8); \
	VOL="kapow-dev-pgdata-$$HASH"; \
	CONT="kapow-dev-$$HASH"; \
	docker rm -f "$$CONT" 2>/dev/null || true; \
	if docker volume inspect "$$VOL" >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		docker volume rm "$$VOL"; \
		echo "Removed volume $$VOL"; \
	else \
		echo "Volume $$VOL does not exist"; \
	fi

## dev-db-up: Start standalone dev database (PostgreSQL+PostGIS on port 5433, full dataset)
dev-db-up:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml up -d --wait
	@echo ""
	@echo "Dev database starting on port 5433. Use:"
	@echo "  DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/kapow?sslmode=disable"

## dev-db-down: Stop and remove the standalone dev database container
dev-db-down:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml down

## compose-up: Start 3-service production topology (db + api + web) on http://localhost:8080
compose-up:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml up -d --build --wait

## compose-down: Stop and remove compose services and volumes
compose-down:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml down -v

## compose-logs: Tail logs from all compose services
compose-logs:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml logs -f

## dev-prune: Remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days
dev-prune:
	@CUTOFF=$$(date -d '30 days ago' +%s 2>/dev/null || date -v-30d +%s); \
	for vol in $$(docker volume ls --filter label=kapow-dev=true -q 2>/dev/null); do \
		CREATED=$$(docker volume inspect "$$vol" --format '{{.CreatedAt}}' 2>/dev/null); \
		VOL_TS=$$(date -d "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S" "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0); \
		if [ "$$VOL_TS" -lt "$$CUTOFF" ]; then \
			echo "Removing old volume: $$vol (created $$CREATED)"; \
			docker volume rm "$$vol" 2>/dev/null || true; \
		fi; \
	done; \
	echo "Prune complete."

## kapow-build: Build the kapow data pipeline binary
kapow-build:
	cd tools/kapow && go build -o ../../bin/kapow .

## kapow-test: Run all kapow tool tests
kapow-test:
	cd tools/kapow && go test ./...

## kapow-test-unit: Run kapow unit tests only (no DB)
kapow-test-unit:
	cd tools/kapow && go test -short ./...

## tools-build: Build all tools (samplesql, kapow)
tools-build: kapow-build
	cd tools/samplesql && go build -o ../../bin/samplesql .

## tools-test: Run tests for all tools
tools-test: kapow-test
	cd tools/samplesql && go test ./...

## test-quick: Fast checks — Go unit tests, TS type-check + build, ESLint (~10-20s, no DB)
test-quick:
	MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test-unit web-build web-lint

## test-standard: Full local validation — quick + web unit tests + Go lint (~30-60s, no DB)
test-standard:
	$(MAKE) test-quick && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh web-test api-lint

## test-fixture: Validate fixture data loads cleanly against current schema (spins up temp Docker PG)
test-fixture:
	bash scripts/test-fixture.sh

## test-full: Complete validation — standard + integration, e2e, smoke, fixture (requires DB + running services)
test-full:
	$(MAKE) test-standard && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test test-fixture tools-test && $(MAKE) web-test-e2e && $(MAKE) smoke-test

## fixture-sql: Generate complete.sql.xz from JSON fixture data (requires Docker)
fixture-sql:
	bash scripts/gen-fixture-sql.sh

## fixture-json: Re-extract JSON fixture data from a live database (requires DATABASE_URL)
fixture-json:
	cd tools/samplesql && go run . --format json --output-dir ../../common/src/main/resources/data/tables

## spec-gen: Generate reference documentation in docs/specs/generated/
spec-gen:
	bash scripts/gen-specs.sh

## fixture-sql: Generate complete.sql.xz from JSON fixture data (requires Docker)
fixture-sql:
	bash scripts/gen-fixture-sql.sh

## fixture-json: Re-extract JSON fixture data from a live database (requires DATABASE_URL)
fixture-json:
	cd tools/samplesql && go run . --format json --output-dir ../../common/src/main/resources/data/tables

## help: List all available targets
help:
	@ESSENTIAL="api-generate build-full dev help lint test-image-run test-full test-quick test-standard web-schema-sync"; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "Usage: make <target>"; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "Essential targets:"; \
	echo ""; \
	grep -hE '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then \
					printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
					break; \
				fi; \
			done; \
		done; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "All targets:"; \
	echo ""; \
	grep -hE '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			SKIP=false; \
			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then SKIP=true; break; fi; \
			done; \
			if [ "$$SKIP" = "false" ]; then \
				printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
			fi; \
		done

# Plan: Agent Workflow Documentation (kapow-c0s.4)

## Context

Coding agents working on Kapow need a single reference document that tells them
the exact workflow from task pickup to merge — including which commands to run,
when to update contracts, and what surfaces require extra care. The information
exists scattered across CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, api/CLAUDE.md, web/CLAUDE.md, and
contract-gates.md, but there is no unified step-by-step guide.

## Approach

Create `docs/specs/agent-workflow.md` — a concise, actionable reference that
synthesizes the workflow from existing docs without duplicating their detail
(links back to source docs instead).

## Document outline

1. **Workflow overview** — numbered steps from task pickup to merge
2. **Quality gate commands by change type** — table mapping change scope (Go, Web, Both, GraphQL, DB migration) to required commands
3. **Contract update rules** — when agents must update contract files (specs, CLAUDE.md, tests, fixtures)
4. **Protected surfaces** — list of files/patterns that need extra care (generated files, migrations, fixtures, brand colors, SQL)
5. **Responding to verification failures** — decision tree for handling failures
6. **Commit and merge protocol** — conventions, worktree merging, session completion

## Files to create

- `docs/specs/agent-workflow.md` — the new document

## Files referenced (not modified)

- `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md` — workflow rules, git conventions
- `api/CLAUDE.md`, `web/CLAUDE.md` — component-specific conventions
- `docs/specs/contract-gates.md` — test tiers and gate behavior
- `Makefile` — verification commands

## Verification

- `make test-quick` — docs-only change, should pass unchanged

# Agent Workflow

Step-by-step reference for coding agents working on the Kapow monorepo.
Covers the full lifecycle from task pickup through merge.

Related specs: [Contract Gates](contract-gates.md), [Search Engine](search-engine.md),
[GraphQL API](graphql-api.md), [Data Model](data-model.md).

---

## Workflow overview

1. **Pick up work** — `bd ready` to find unblocked issues; `bd update <id> --status in_progress` to claim.
2. **Read context** — Read `CLAUDE.md`, component CLAUDE files, and any linked plan in `docs/plans/`.
3. **Plan** — For non-trivial work (3+ steps), enter plan mode. Save approved plans to `docs/plans/<issue-id>-<short-name>.md`.
4. **Implement** — Write code and tests together. Follow conventions in the component CLAUDE files.
5. **Verify** — Run the quality gate commands for your change type (see table below). All must pass.
6. **Update contracts** — If your change affects protected surfaces, update the relevant contract files (see below).
7. **Commit** — Follow commit conventions in `AGENTS.md`. Reference issue IDs when `.beads/` files change.
8. **Merge to main** — Merge directly from CLI. No PR unless there are unresolvable conflicts.
9. **Close issue** — `bd close <id>` then `bd sync && git push`.

---

## Quality gate commands by change type

Run the commands for every area your change touches. Each row includes all
previous tiers.

| Change type | Minimum gate | Commands |
|---|---|---|
| **Go only** | `make test-quick` | `make api-test-unit` |
| **Web only** | `make test-quick` | `make web-build && make web-lint` |
| **Go + Web** | `make test-standard` | `make test-quick && make web-test && make api-lint` |
| **GraphQL schema** | `make test-standard` | Above + `make api-generate && make web-schema-sync` (check for uncommitted diff) |
| **DB migration** | `make test-full` | Above + `make test-fixture && make api-test` (requires DB) |
| **Search field** | `make test-standard` | `make api-test-unit` covers contract matrix + field coverage automatically |
| **Docs only** | `make test-quick` | Smoke check — ensures no accidental code breakage |

### Pre-merge checklist

Every branch must pass `make test-standard` before merging to `main`.
If the change touches database code or fixtures, also run `make test-full`.
See [Contract Gates — Pre-merge checklist](contract-gates.md#pre-merge-checklist) for the full list.

---

## Contract update rules

When your change affects one of these areas, you **must** update the
corresponding contract file in the same commit.

| What changed | Update required |
|---|---|
| New/modified search field | Test case auto-covered by `TestBuildSQLAllFields`; verify it passes |
| New GraphQL query/mutation | Add resolver tests + `IntegrationSearch_*` test if search-related |
| New UI component in `ui/` | Add `<component>.test.tsx` alongside it |
| Schema change (`*.graphql`) | `make api-generate && make web-schema-sync`; commit generated files |
| DB migration | Update fixture COPY headers if affected table is in fixture data; `make test-fixture` |
| New env var | Add to `Config` struct with `env:` tag + document in `.env.example` |
| Behaviour change to documented feature | Update the relevant spec in `docs/specs/` |
| New contract gate | Add row to [contract-gates.md](contract-gates.md) tables |

**Core rule**: tests and contract updates ship with the code, never as a follow-up.

---

## Protected surfaces

These files and patterns require extra care. Mistakes here are hard to
reverse or cause cascading failures.

| Surface | Rule | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `graph/generated/`, `graph/model/models_gen.go` | **Never edit manually** — run `make api-generate` | Overwritten on next generation |
| `web/schema.graphql`, `web/src/graphql-env.d.ts` | **Never edit manually** — run `make web-schema-sync` | Overwritten on next sync |
| `migrations/*.sql` | **Never modify a shipped migration** — create a new one | Applied migrations are immutable in production |
| Fixture data (`common/src/main/resources/data/`) | Update COPY headers when schema changes | Stale fixtures break `make test-fixture` |
| SQL queries | **Always use `$N` placeholders** — never `fmt.Sprintf` | SQL injection risk |
| Brand colors | **Never hardcode hex** — use Mantine refs or CSS vars | Inconsistent theming |
| `.env` | **Never commit** — only `.env.example` | Secrets exposure |
| `node_modules/`, `dist/` | **Never commit** | Repository bloat |

---

## Responding to verification failures

When a quality gate command fails:

1. **Read the error output carefully.** Identify whether it is your change or a pre-existing issue.
2. **If your change caused it** — fix the code. Do not waive your own failures.
3. **If pre-existing / flaky** — follow the [waiver process](contract-gates.md#waiver-paths):
   - Document the failure in the commit message.
   - File a beads issue (`bd create`) to track the fix.
   - The waiver must be resolved within 5 business days.
4. **If a generated file is stale** — regenerate:
   - Go/GraphQL: `make api-generate`
   - Web schema: `make web-schema-sync`
   - Reference docs: `make spec-gen`
5. **If fixture data is incompatible** — update fixture COPY headers to match the current schema, then `make test-fixture`.
6. **Never skip hooks or bypass gates** (`--no-verify`, etc.) to force a merge.
7. **If stuck** — stop and re-plan. Do not brute-force past failures.

---

## Commit and merge protocol

### Commit conventions

- Use conventional commit prefixes: `feat:`, `fix:`, `docs:`, `test:`, `refactor:`, `chore:`.
- When `.beads/` files are included, list affected issue IDs in the body: `Issues: kapow-abc, kapow-def`.
- End with `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>` for agent-authored commits.

### Merge workflow

1. Run the appropriate quality gate (see table above) — all checks must pass.
2. Merge directly into `main` from the CLI. No pull request unless conflicts cannot be resolved.
3. For worktree branches: always merge back to `main` before ending the session.
4. After merging a branch that touched GraphQL schema files: `make api-generate && make web-schema-sync`.

### Session completion (mandatory)

Every session must end with all work pushed to remote. See
[AGENTS.md — Landing the Plane](../../AGENTS.md#landing-the-plane-session-completion)
for the full checklist. The critical sequence:

```bash
git pull --rebase
bd sync
git push
git status  # must show "up to date with origin"
```

Work is **not complete** until `git push` succeeds.
